title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The API server pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the API server. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
input_row.default_value: By default, the kube-apiserver.yaml file has permissions of 640 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions a command to check file permissions on the Control Plane node, which implies that it is a property that can be verified through Kubernetes APIs or kubectl commands.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of file permissions for maintaining the integrity of the API server pod specification file, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- Since the permissions of the kube-apiserver.yaml file can be checked programmatically, this control can be evaluated automatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_pod_spec_file_permissions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to check the permissions of the kube-apiserver.yaml file located at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`.
- The command to run would be:
  ```bash
  kubectl exec -n kube-system $(kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
  ```
- This command retrieves the permissions of the kube-apiserver.yaml file.
- The expected Pass criteria is that the permissions should be `644` or more restrictive (e.g., `600`).
- If the output is `644` or less (e.g., `600`), the check passes; otherwise, it fails.
